_source: Environment (Protection) Rules, 1986 — Schedule VI, Part-A: Effluents (CPCB General Standards for Discharge of Environmental Pollutants). VERIFIED 2026-07-05 line-by-line against the primary-source gazette PDF (data/reference/general-standards/GeneralStandards.pdf). Columns model Inland surface water and Public sewers only (the app's two destinations); Land-for-irrigation and Marine coastal columns exist in the gazette but are omitted here. Factory CTO / SPCB limits OVERRIDE these and are often stricter.

_notes.bod: BOD basis is 3 days at 27 C. Gazette footnote (G.S.R.176, 2.4.1996): read as BOD (3 days at 27 C) wherever 'BOD 5 days 20 C' appears.
_notes.temperature: General standard is a DELTA: inland = 'shall not exceed 5 C above receiving-water temperature'; public sewers = NO general limit ('--' in the gazette). The numeric max values below are pragmatic absolute PROXIES for screening only — set the real limit from the factory CTO.
_notes.tds: TDS does NOT appear anywhere in Schedule VI Part-A. It is sector/CTO-specific (e.g. distillery, pharma). Value below is a flagged CTO placeholder, not a general standard.
_notes.fecal_coliform: Not in Schedule VI Part-A. Coliform limits come from IS:2296 / STP norms. Deliberately omitted.

tds._note: NOT a CPCB general standard — set from factory CTO

temperature.rule: no CPCB general limit for sewer temp

tds._note: NOT a CPCB general standard — set from factory CTO
